title: Skip and Residual Redesign
research_question: If the current residual family is useful but over-parameterized, what cleaner routing design preserves the benefit with less baggage?

candidates.name: m1-shared-skip-gate
candidates.goal: Replace per-channel learned skip weights with one shared scalar gate per skip link.
candidates.hypothesis: The model may want skip topology and coarse routing strength, but not full per-channel skip parameterization.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the skip-weight win from tranche H is mostly about having any gate at all.
candidates.why_worthy: Tranche H already showed learned skip weights are the softest target in this family.
candidates.description: use shared scalar skip gates instead of full learned skip-weight vectors

candidates.name: m2-sparse-skip-pattern
candidates.goal: Keep skip topology but reduce the number of skip links.
candidates.hypothesis: The model may only need a few strategically placed skip paths rather than the full current skip pattern.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether skip density matters as much as skip existence.
candidates.why_worthy: This tests topology redesign, not just parameterization changes.
candidates.description: keep only alternating encoder-decoder skip links with shared scalar gates

candidates.name: m3-scalar-resid-mix
candidates.goal: Replace full per-channel resid_mix with one scalar or one small shared gate.
candidates.hypothesis: The model may need the residual stream blend concept, but not full per-channel freedom for it.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether `resid_mix` is valuable because of its mechanism or because of its parameter count.
candidates.why_worthy: Tranche H showed resid_mix matters, so the next worthwhile question is how much structure it really needs.
candidates.description: replace full per-channel resid_mix with a shared scalar gate

candidates.name: m4-shared-resid-scales
candidates.goal: Keep residual scaling but share it more aggressively.
candidates.hypothesis: The model may want residual scaling as a concept while not needing one learned scale per channel.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether residual scales are about coarse branch weighting or fine-grained feature control.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the clean follow-up to tranche H's 'scales matter' result.
candidates.description: replace per-channel attn/mlp residual scales with one shared scalar each

candidates.name: m5-cheap-routing-package
candidates.goal: Test one coherent cheaper routing system built from the best redesign ideas.
candidates.hypothesis: A cleaner routing package with shared gates may preserve most of the residual-family benefit while cutting needless flexibility.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the family wants redesign as a system rather than one knob at a time.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the only justified combo run because the routing components likely interact strongly.
candidates.description: combine shared scalar skip gates, scalar resid_mix, and shared residual scales
